hero.question: Will Roberto Sánchez Palomino win the 2026 Peruvian presidential election?
hero.prob_take: At 30.4%, the market is pricing Sánchez Palomino as a clear third or fourth choice in a crowded field, despite a $5.1M spike suggesting some traders smell something the consensus hasn't priced yet. The 11-point overnight move feels reactive to polling noise rather than structural shifts, making this a fade unless Peru's political fragmentation suddenly crystallizes around an outsider.

daily_take.headline: Peru's Dark Horse Just Jumped 11 Points. Smart Money Knows Why.
daily_take.deck: Roberto Sánchez Palomino rocketed from 19% to 30.4% in 24 hours on fresh momentum, but the market's pricing him like a coronation is certain. The real alpha lives in the spread. López Aliaga collapsed 19 points in the same window, signaling a crowded trade unwinding, not new conviction. Take the contrarian side of the move.

sidebar.headline: López Aliaga's free-fall looks like panic selling more than polling deterioration. The 19-point cliff is algorithmic, not fundamental.
sidebar.label: Rafael López Aliaga: 5.5%

sidebar.headline: Borges jumped 26 points in 24 hours on match day volatility. Check the injury report before chasing momentum into match time.
sidebar.label: Borges vs Etcheverry: 58.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic bet ticked 6 points on regional tension. Iran's latest saber-rattling has real optionality, but 24.5% underprices geopolitical tail risk.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal by April: 24.5%
